mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes‑Benz CLK55 / CLK63 AMG engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned CLK55 AMG (M113) engine typically costs £4,000–£7,500 supply only, while a CLK63 AMG (M156) engine costs £6,000–£12,000. A CLK63 AMG engine replacement can cost approximately £4,000–£8,000 from a specialist. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800 at independent specialist rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes‑Benz CLK55 / CLK63 AMG engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on which engine you're asking about. The M113 (CLK55) has a legendary 'bulletproof' reputation, but age takes its toll: rear crank seal leaks, valve cover gaskets, and coil pack failures are common. The M156 (CLK63) is a masterpiece of engineering but has well‑documented weaknesses: cylinder head bolt failure, camshaft lobe and valve lifter wear, and valve train noise. Regular maintenance is absolutely essential.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes‑Benz CLK55 / CLK63 AMG?

acceptedAnswer.text: CLK55 (M113): rear crank seal leaks, valve cover and breather leaks, coil pack and MAF sensor failures. CLK63 (M156): cylinder head bolt failure, camshaft adjuster and valve train wear, valve bucket 'bleed down' causing cold‑start tapping, crankcase breather valve failure, oil leaks, and thermostat failures. See the Era Map above for generation‑specific advice.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a CLK55 / CLK63 AMG engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well‑kept CLK63 AMG (values £15,000–£35,000+), a £6,000–£8,000 reconditioned engine is 20–50% of the vehicle's value — often worth it. The CLK55 AMG (values £8,000–£20,000+) makes a £4,000–£5,500 recon a 25–50% investment. Given the CLK AMG's status as a future classic and rising values, engine replacement is often worth it for a good example.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes‑Benz CLK55 / CLK63 AMG engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code (M113.987 or M156) is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The CLK55 AMG (2003–2005) uses the M113.987 5.4‑litre V8 producing 362 bhp. The CLK63 AMG (2006–2009) uses the M156 6.2‑litre V8 producing 475 bhp. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the M156 head bolts, or does it require engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative head bolt replacement with updated ARP studs costs £2,000–£5,000 and is widely considered essential preventative maintenance on the M156. However, if the head bolts have failed and caused cylinder head damage, a full engine replacement may be required. If you're buying a CLK63 AMG, check whether this work has been done.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes‑Benz CLK55 / CLK63 AMG Engine Replacement Costs

rows.bestFor: Budget priority; finding a good donor M113/M156 is becoming harder
M113 and M156 engines are no longer produced new; reconditioned units are the standard

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the CLK55/CLK63 AMG era (2003–2009). A CLK63 AMG engine replacement can cost approximately £4,000–£8,000 from a specialist. One owner reported a quote of £8,000 for a fitted engine, with a used M156 engine and transmission available from around £4,100.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a CLK AMG engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £85–£130/hr (independent) or £140–£200/hr (London/SE dealer). The V8's size and complexity add significant labour. [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]

cta.label: → Compare CLK55 / CLK63 AMG engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: M156 Cylinder Head Bolt Failure (CLK63 AMG)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss, overheating, white smoke from exhaust, check engine light, loss of power, misfires, potential catastrophic engine failure.
problems.repairCost: £2,000–£5,000 (head bolt replacement with updated ARP studs — often requires cylinder head removal)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you notice coolant loss or overheating, stop driving immediately

problems.recommendation: The M156's cylinder head studs can stretch, allowing the cylinder head to lift slightly and causing coolant to leak into the combustion chamber or oil passages. This is the single biggest reliability concern on the CLK63 AMG. Symptoms include coolant loss, overheating, white smoke, and engine damage. If you suspect head bolt issues, have a leak‑down test performed immediately. Many owners proactively replace the factory head bolts with updated ARP studs to prevent failure. If you're buying a CLK63, check whether this work has been done.

problems.title: M113 Rear Crank Seal & Valve Cover Leaks (CLK55 AMG)
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the vehicle, burning oil smell, low oil warning, visible oil seepage around the rear of the engine or valve covers.
problems.repairCost: £400–£1,200 (valve cover gaskets) · £800–£2,000 (rear crank seal — requires transmission removal)【PLACEHOLDER】
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (unless oil starvation leads to engine damage)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; oil leaks can damage other components

problems.recommendation: The rear crank seal is a very common issue on the M113. Both valve cover and breather leaks are also common — they "do drip into unexpected places". One owner reported that after replacing the rear crank seal, it started leaking again within 6–8 months until a modified seal arrangement was used. If you notice oil spots under the car or a burning smell, have the system inspected promptly. The valve cover and breather leaks are "cheap enough to fix".

problems.title: M156 Camshaft Adjuster & Valve Train Wear (CLK63 AMG)
problems.symptoms: Loud tapping or rattling noise on cold start, rough idling, engine knocking noises especially on startup, reduced engine performance, check engine light.
problems.replacementCost: £6,000–£12,000 (if failure leads to valve train damage)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear tapping or rattling, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The M156 is known for accelerated wear in the valvetrain, with valve buckets prone to "bleed down" resulting in excessive valve lash during cold start — commonly heard as a loud tapping noise until the engine reaches operating temperature. Early production engines have noisy valve "buckets", cam wear issues, and head bolts that just aren't able to endure sustained performance driving. If you hear unusual noises from the top of the engine, have the valvetrain inspected immediately.

problems.title: M113 Coil Pack & MAF Sensor Failure (CLK55 AMG)
problems.symptoms: Misfires, loss of power, rough running, check engine light, poor fuel economy.

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; can affect drivability and fuel economy

problems.recommendation: Coil packs and MAF sensors "can be an issue" on the M113. The M113 is generally bulletproof as long as it is serviced properly, but these sensors can fail over time. If you experience misfires or rough running, have the ignition system and MAF sensor inspected. Replace all coil packs preventatively to avoid catalytic converter damage from prolonged misfires.

problems.title: M156 Thermostat & Cooling System Issues (CLK63 AMG)
problems.symptoms: Engine fan constantly running on high, coolant temperature error message on dash, vehicle taking longer than usual to get to operating temperature.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (unless overheating leads to engine damage)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if cooling system warnings appear

problems.recommendation: Thermostat failure on the M156 can range from minor annoyances to critical issues. The most prevalent symptoms include the engine fan running constantly, dashboard temperature warnings, and slow warm‑up. If you notice any of these, have the cooling system inspected promptly to prevent more serious issues.

problems.title: M156 Crankcase Breather Valve Failure (CLK63 AMG)
problems.symptoms: Increased oil consumption, misfires, heavy smoke from tailpipe, check engine light (P0170), rough running.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (unless oil starvation leads to engine damage)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; can lead to increased oil consumption and misfires

problems.recommendation: The M156's crankcase breather valve is a common failure point. The diaphragm on the valve deteriorates, or the hose from the crankcase becomes brittle and cracks. This leads to increased oil burning, misfires, and heavy smoke. A maintenance interval of around 60,000 miles is recommended for replacement. The job involves removing the intake manifold and takes about 30 minutes.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Mercedes‑Benz CLK55 / CLK63 AMG — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.problem: M113 rear crank seal / valve cover leaks
M113 coil pack / MAF sensor failure

rows.whenItMakesSense: Proactive ARP stud upgrade prevents catastrophic failure

rows.problem: M156 camshaft adjuster / valve train wear
rows.whenItMakesSense: Catch it early — far cheaper than engine replacement
Straightforward replacement; catch it before overheating
M156 rebuilds are exceptionally expensive; assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This CLK55 / CLK63 AMG? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with regular oil changes using Mercedes‑approved oil
* CLK55 (M113): no oil leaks from rear crank seal or valve covers
* CLK63 (M156): head bolts replaced with updated ARP studs (critical)
* CLK63 (M156): no cold‑start tapping from the top of the engine
* CLK63 (M156): crankcase breather valve replaced (60,000‑mile maintenance)
* No check engine lights on either engine
avoidIf: * No proof of regular maintenance — these V8s are unforgiving of neglect
* CLK63 with no head bolt work — the "fearsome issue" is likely to appear
* CLK63 with cold‑start tapping — camshaft adjuster or valve train wear is likely
* Any coolant loss or overheating — head bolt issues or head gasket failure
* Any engine warning light without diagnosis — these engines can hide expensive faults
* "Cheap" examples — the purchase price is the least of your worries

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Mercedes‑Benz CLK55 and CLK63 AMG represent the pinnacle of naturally aspirated AMG V8 engineering from two different eras. The CLK55's M113 is one of Mercedes' most reliable V8s — the 5.4‑litre engine has a legendary "bulletproof" reputation with "bullet proof" reliability. However, age takes its toll: rear crank seal leaks, valve cover gaskets, and coil pack failures are common maintenance items.

cta.label: → Compare CLK55 / CLK63 AMG engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes‑Benz CLK55 or CLK63 AMG (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for tapping or rattling (M156 camshaft adjuster / valve train warning; M113 timing chain tensioner)
* Service History — Regular oil changes are absolutely essential for both engines
* Oil Leaks (CLK55) — Check for oil around the rear of the engine (rear crank seal) and valve covers
* Head Bolt Work (CLK63) — Has the car had head bolts replaced with updated ARP studs? (critical)
* Coolant Level — Check cold and hot; any loss indicates potential head bolt or cooling system issues
* Valve Train (CLK63) — Any unusual noise from the top of the engine?
* Crankcase Breather (CLK63) — Has it been replaced at 60,000 miles?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Misfire, MAF, or cooling system codes are most common

items.label: SECTION 8 — FAQ
items.value: 1. How much does a Mercedes‑Benz CLK55 / CLK63 AMG engine replacement cost in the UK?

items.label: A reconditioned CLK55 AMG (M113) engine typically costs £4,000–£7,500 supply only, while a CLK63 AMG (M156) engine costs £6,000–£12,000. A CLK63 AMG engine replacement can cost approximately £4,000–£8,000 from a specialist, with one owner reporting a quote of £8,000 for a fitted engine. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800 at independent specialist rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.
items.value: 2. Is the Mercedes‑Benz CLK55 / CLK63 AMG engine reliable?

items.label: It depends on which engine you're asking about. The M113 (CLK55) has a legendary "bulletproof" reputation — one owner said they bought the car "because of its reputation as a trouble free, bullet proof engine". However, age takes its toll: rear crank seal leaks, valve cover gaskets, and coil pack failures are common.
items.value: The M156 (CLK63) is a different story — it's a masterpiece of engineering but has well‑documented weaknesses: cylinder head bolt failure (the "fearsome issue"), camshaft lobe and valve lifter wear, and valve train noise. One owner described AMG V8s as "generally grenades".

items.label: 3. What are the common problems with the Mercedes‑Benz CLK55 / CLK63 AMG?
items.value: CLK55 (M113): rear crank seal leaks, valve cover and breather leaks, coil pack and MAF sensor failures. CLK63 (M156): cylinder head bolt failure, camshaft adjuster and valve train wear, valve bucket "bleed down" causing cold‑start tapping, crankcase breather valve failure, oil leaks from valve cover gaskets, and thermostat failures. See the Era Map above for generation‑specific advice.

items.label: 4. Is it worth replacing a CLK55 / CLK63 AMG engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.value: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well‑kept CLK63 AMG (values £15,000–£35,000+ depending on condition and mileage), a £6,000–£8,000 reconditioned engine is 20–50% of the vehicle's value — often worth it for a solid example. The CLK55 AMG (values £8,000–£20,000+) makes a £4,000–£5,500 recon a 25–50% investment — usually worth it. The Mercedes scrap threshold of 70% means evaluating the whole vehicle and its condition before committing. Given the CLK AMG's status as a future classic and rising values, engine replacement is often worth it for a good example.

items.label: 5. How do I find my Mercedes‑Benz CLK55 / CLK63 AMG engine code?
items.value: The engine code (M113.987 or M156) is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The CLK55 AMG (2003–2005) uses the M113.987 5.4‑litre V8 producing 362 bhp. The CLK63 AMG (2006–2009) uses the M156 6.2‑litre V8 producing 475 bhp. The CLK55 was discontinued in 2005 and replaced by the CLK63 for the 2006 model year. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm for free.

items.label: 6. Can I repair the M156 head bolts, or does it require engine replacement?
items.value: Yes — if caught early. A preventative head bolt replacement with updated ARP studs costs £2,000–£5,000 and is widely considered essential preventative maintenance on the M156. However, if the head bolts have failed and caused cylinder head damage, a full engine replacement (£6,000–£12,000) may be required. If you're buying a CLK63 AMG, check whether this work has been done — it's the single most important question to ask.

items.label: Disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data from our network of engine specialists. Actual costs vary by variant, engine code, damage severity and workshop location. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes. Every engine supplied through EnginesMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited‑mileage warranty — always confirm terms with your chosen supplier.
